summary: 0 ERRORs require a code-fix script. 6 unaddressed ERRORs split into three categories: (1) intentional NotImplementedError placeholders awaiting upstream prerequisites (V3-EXQ-455a, V3-EXQ-449c) -- these are not bugs and were already flagged in the 2026-04-29 staging report; (2) coincident SIGTERM (exit -15) on both cloud workers at 2026-05-06T09:28Z (V3-EXQ-530 on ree-cloud-1, V3-EXQ-244a on ree-cloud-2) plus a prior SIGTERM (V3-EXQ-495 on ree-cloud-1, 2026-04-28T21:13Z) -- infrastructure failures, no code fix; (3) one stale March-2026 SD-003 attempt (V3-EXQ-008) functionally superseded by the 27-experiment SD-003 program (anchor V3-EXQ-525 PASSed today). Note: V3-EXQ-449c, V3-EXQ-455a, V3-EXQ-008 also appeared in the 2026-04-29 staging report's errors_already_discussed_no_action list -- carryover for review_tracker handoff.

diagnoses.root_cause: March 17 2026 SD-003 larger-world / smaller-observation probe exited code 1 in 0.9s. Pre-runner.log retention period; traceback unrecoverable from local logs. SD-003 has had 27 follow-on experiments since then; V3-EXQ-525 PASSed as the SD-003 anchor 2026-05-06T06:51Z.
diagnoses.fix_applied: None. Scientific question functionally absorbed into the SD-003 program via different EXQ numbers; no longer load-bearing under this ID. Already listed in the 2026-04-29 staging report's errors_already_discussed_no_action -- carryover for review_tracker handoff.

diagnoses.root_cause: Script raises NotImplementedError at top of run with self-documenting message: 'V3-EXQ-455a full implementation pending: awaiting V3-EXQ-476 cascade gate PASS and MECH-284 Phase 3 consumer landing.' Exit 1 in 0.05s. Intentional precondition gate, not a bug.
diagnoses.fix_applied: None. V3-EXQ-476 is itself a NotImplementedError placeholder; successors V3-EXQ-476a and V3-EXQ-476b both UNKNOWN. Until MECH-284 Phase 3 consumer + V_s flag plumbing in REEConfig.from_dims() are complete, no work is appropriate on V3-EXQ-455a. Already listed in the 2026-04-29 staging report -- carryover for review_tracker handoff.
diagnoses.claim_ids_rationale: No fix to claim against; gate experiment will determine claim_ids when prerequisites land.

diagnoses.root_cause: Script raises NotImplementedError at top of run with self-documenting message: 'V3-EXQ-449c full implementation pending: awaiting (a) V3-EXQ-476 PASS, (b) V3-EXQ-445d PASS, and (c) MECH-074b hippocampal consumer wiring landed on SD-035.' Exit 1 in 0.1s. Intentional gate.
diagnoses.fix_applied: None. All three prerequisites unmet: V3-EXQ-476 ERROR (placeholder), V3-EXQ-445d ERROR (also a placeholder awaiting V3-EXQ-476), MECH-074b retrieval-bias-aware replay path not yet present in SD-035. Pure dependency chain. Already listed in the 2026-04-29 staging report -- carryover for review_tracker handoff.

diagnoses.root_cause: MECH-163 V3 full-completion gate (VTA / hippocampally-planned arm) ran 4.0 hours on ree-cloud-1 starting 2026-04-28T17:14Z, then exit -15 (SIGTERM) at 21:13Z. SIGTERM is an external signal -- cloud worker process killed by parent (timeout, restart, or remote shutdown). No Python traceback.
diagnoses.fix_applied: None. Not a code bug. The estimate budget for MECH-163 (heavyweight full-completion gate) likely exceeded the cloud worker's runtime ceiling, or the worker was restarted mid-run. Recommend: (a) confirm whether still scientifically wanted, (b) if yes, re-queue with force_rerun=true OR fresh letter (V3-EXQ-495a) and pin machine_affinity to a host with sufficient runtime headroom (DLAPTOP-4.local typically OK for long runs).
diagnoses.claim_ids_rationale: Inherited from predecessor since the script never produced measurement -- SIGTERM tells us nothing about scope. If a human re-runs, claim_ids should be re-evaluated against the actual script body.

diagnoses.root_cause: ARC-016 precision-to-commitment circuit ran 10.0 minutes on ree-cloud-1 starting 2026-05-06T09:18:48Z, then SIGTERM at 09:28:51Z. ree-cloud-1 last heartbeat 09:16:44Z (idle). Coincident with V3-EXQ-244a SIGTERM on ree-cloud-2 at 09:28:53Z (within 2 seconds) -- simultaneous shutdown of both cloud workers around 09:28-29Z today.
diagnoses.fix_applied: None. This experiment was smoke-tested PASS earlier today (~70s smoke run, all code paths exercised; per WORKSPACE_STATE 'write-exq530-arc016-precision-commit 2026-05-06T09:18Z'). The 10-minute SIGTERM is purely an infrastructure event, not a script regression. Recommend: re-queue under same ID with force_rerun=true, OR assign V3-EXQ-530a; consider machine_affinity adjustment if cloud restart pattern repeats.
diagnoses.claim_ids_rationale: Inherited from queued predecessor -- SIGTERM produced no measurement, so claim_ids unchanged from original design (ARC-016 dACC precision_commit_ratio). Re-evaluate at re-queue time only if script is modified.

diagnoses.root_cause: MECH-165 reverse replay diversity validation ran 1.34 hours on ree-cloud-2 starting 2026-05-06T08:08:44Z, then SIGTERM at 09:28:53Z. ree-cloud-2 last heartbeat 08:06:40Z (idle). Coincident with V3-EXQ-530 SIGTERM on ree-cloud-1 at 09:28:51Z. Same cloud-shutdown event.
diagnoses.fix_applied: None. There is a flat-JSON evidence file at REE_assembly/evidence/experiments/v3_exq_244a_mech165_replay_diversity_validation_v3.json with outcome=PASS, but its run_id has no timestamp suffix and the file is untracked in git -- this is a smoke-test artifact from local dry-run, NOT a real production run. condition_stats show NO_REPLAY/FORWARD_REPLAY retentions of -9.3e-11 (zero noise) and BALANCED_REPLAY at 0.013 driven by a single seed -- implausibly clean for a real run, consistent with a smoke-test artifact. Recommend: (a) delete the smoke-test JSON before re-running so it cannot be mistaken for evidence, (b) re-queue under same ID with force_rerun=true, OR assign V3-EXQ-244b.
diagnoses.claim_ids_rationale: Inherited from predecessor -- SIGTERM produced no real measurement. Re-evaluate only if script body is modified.

infrastructure_observation.signature: Both cloud workers issued SIGTERM to active runs within 2 seconds of each other. Heartbeats from both machines are stale (last ticks 08:06Z and 09:16Z respectively).
infrastructure_observation.recommendation: Investigate whether ree-cloud workers were intentionally stopped or hit a shared host-side limit. If unintentional, restart the runners with --auto-sync --loop --remote-control. The same incident also explains why no later runs by either cloud worker appear in runner_status. The 2026-04-28T21:13Z V3-EXQ-495 SIGTERM on ree-cloud-1 is the same pattern from earlier (cloud worker kills mid-run); recurring infra problem worth flagging.
infrastructure_observation.carryover_observation: The 2026-04-29 staging report flagged duplicate-machine artifacts (V3-EXQ-125a / V3-EXQ-247 / V3-EXQ-249b / V3-EXQ-498) for governance handoff to review_tracker.discussed_experiment_dirs. None of those have been absorbed into review_tracker yet (the 2026-04-29 next_actions remain open). Today's run identifies three more candidates (V3-EXQ-008, V3-EXQ-455a, V3-EXQ-449c) already noted in that prior report as errors_already_discussed_no_action.

next_actions: Governance handoff: add V3-EXQ-008, V3-EXQ-455a, V3-EXQ-449c, V3-EXQ-495, V3-EXQ-530, V3-EXQ-244a to review_tracker.discussed_experiment_dirs in the next /governance walk so they stop appearing as undiagnosed ERRORs.

User decision: confirm whether V3-EXQ-495 / V3-EXQ-530 / V3-EXQ-244a should be re-queued. For V3-EXQ-244a, delete the smoke-test JSON at evidence/experiments/v3_exq_244a_mech165_replay_diversity_validation_v3.json BEFORE re-queuing so it is not mistaken for evidence.

Infra: investigate the 2026-05-06T09:28Z coincident SIGTERM on ree-cloud-1 and ree-cloud-2 (heartbeats stale; restart runners with --auto-sync --loop --remote-control if workers are still down). Same pattern as 2026-04-28 V3-EXQ-495 SIGTERM and 2026-04-07 V3-EXQ-249b SIGTERM -- recurring cloud worker kill behaviour.
Upstream blockers: V3-EXQ-455a and V3-EXQ-449c will keep ERRORing until V3-EXQ-476 lands (which itself awaits MECH-284 Phase 3 + V_s flag plumbing in REEConfig.from_dims()).
